label: Provide a signature
description: Capture a trusted, auditable signature so a service can confirm who signed, what they signed, and when.
use_when: The outcome is legally or procedurally significant, or fraud prevention is essential.
The service must produce a verifiable record of agreement or approval.
Multiple signers, roles, or asynchronous signing are involved.
do_not_use_when: A simple confirmation or acknowledgement is sufficient.
Signing is not required for the decision — use information collection and normal confirmation instead.

steps.label: Verify identity and authorise to sign (optional)
steps.description: Confirm the user is the intended signatory and is authorised to sign in the chosen role.

steps.label: Review what will be signed
steps.description: Show clear, human-readable content and a summary of consequences. User must be able to confirm or cancel.

steps.label: Choose signature method or role (if applicable)
steps.description: If multiple signing methods or roles are available, let the user select.

steps.label: Apply eSignature
steps.description: Service applies the signature via the eSignature Building Block.

steps.label: Return outcome and confirmations
steps.description: Show status, audit reference, and what happens next.

steps.label: Send confirmation notification
steps.description: Send confirmations to signatory and document owner or requester if different.

ux_considerations.text: Enable save-and-return to sign later, with reminder notifications and expiry where relevant.
Signing must be usable with assistive technology such as screen readers.
Where eSignature is not possible, offer assisted or alternative ways to complete this part of the service.

technical_notes.text: All signature events must be logged with user reference, document reference, timestamp, and method.
Signature operations must be protected via Information Mediation when integrating with external systems.
